title: TKE CKD Note Template - Complete Section Registry
description: All 37 sections across 9 clinical domains. Single source of truth for the AI-era CKD note template.
total_sections_note: 38 entries: sections 0-37 (0 = header, 1-37 = clinical sections). 37 clinical sections + 1 header.

sections.interpretation_prompt: Generate a 5-8 line dashboard summary: CKD stage, eGFR trend, UACR trend, GDMT X/4 compliance, top 3 alerts, key changes since last visit.
Assess kidney function trajectory, proteinuria trend, KFRE risk, and whether current interventions are adequate. Reference KDIGO staging and risk categories.
Summarize hematuria workup status and recommend next steps based on AUA/SUFU guidelines.
Assess stone recurrence risk based on type and 24h urine results. Recommend prevention strategy per AUA guidelines.

sections.condition: gu_history is not empty OR bph == true OR prostate_cancer == true
sections.interpretation_prompt: Summarize relevant GU history and its impact on CKD management.
Assess BP control per SPRINT/KDIGO targets. Evaluate fluid status. Note Daxor BVA results if available. Recommend medication adjustments.
Assess HF status, GDMT optimization, cardiorenal syndrome type, and Furoscix candidacy. Reference AHA/ACC guidelines.
Assess CV risk, statin therapy adequacy, and PCSK9i candidacy for CKD patients per KDIGO/ACC lipid guidelines.
Assess RAAS inhibitor status: on therapy, at max dose, tolerating (K+, Cr). If not on, document reason. Cross-reference medication_safety thresholds.
Assess SGLT2i status. If not on, evaluate eligibility (eGFR >= 20). Document initial eGFR dip if applicable. Confirm sick day rules reviewed.
Assess MRA status. If on finerenone, monitor K+ closely. Evaluate candidacy per FIDELIO/FIGARO criteria. Document potassium binder use if applicable.
Assess GLP-1 RA status per FLOW trial evidence. Evaluate kidney benefit documentation. Track weight response. If not on, document reason and candidacy.
Assess glycemic control vs individualized HbA1c target. Review medication safety (metformin GFR thresholds). Check eye/foot exam status. Note Eversense CGM if applicable.

sections.condition: gout_history is not empty OR uric_acid > 6.0
sections.interpretation_prompt: Assess gout control, uric acid target (<6.0), current therapy adequacy, and Krystexxa candidacy for refractory gout.
Assess obesity impact on CKD progression. Review weight trend, GLP-1 RA candidacy (cross-reference Section 11), and bariatric surgery history.
Assess anemia per KDIGO: Hgb trend, iron status (ferritin/TSAT), ESA need. Flag CKD-related vs other etiologies. Cross-ref fax manager thresholds.
Assess CKD-MBD per KDIGO: PTH trend, calcium/phosphorus balance, Vitamin D status. Recommend medication adjustments (Vit D, binders, calcimimetics).
Assess electrolyte panel and acid-base status. Flag critical values. Ensure bicarb >= 22 per KDIGO. Note pruritus if present. Cross-ref fax manager thresholds.
Assess tobacco/substance use impact on CKD progression. Document cessation counseling. Note vaping status.
Assess NSAID use status. Counsel on avoidance per KDIGO. Cross-ref fax manager NSAID drug list for auto-detection. Recommend alternatives.
Assess PPI use: indicated vs. questionable. Recommend de-escalation if appropriate. Note AIN/CKD progression risk.

sections.condition: on RAAS or SGLT2i or MRA or diuretic
sections.interpretation_prompt: Confirm sick day rules reviewed for all nephrotoxin-risk medications. Generate medication hold list.

sections.condition: contrast_in_30_days == true OR upcoming imaging with contrast
sections.interpretation_prompt: Assess contrast exposure risk per ACR/KDIGO. Document pre-hydration and nephrotoxin hold protocols.
Assess dietary sodium adherence and its impact on BP and fluid status. Recommend dietitian referral if non-adherent.
Assess transplant readiness: candidate status, workup completion, barriers (BMI, cardiac, etc.). Recommend next steps per KDIGO transplant guidelines.
Assess dialysis planning: education status, modality preference, vascular access, timeline. Cross-ref fax manager dialysis planning thresholds. Document vein preservation.
Assess ACP documentation status. Note surprise question answer. Recommend discussion if not documented. Track CPT billing for ACP conversations.
Assess CCM enrollment and activity. Flag care gaps identified by care coordinator. Recommend enrollment if eligible and not enrolled.
Check immunization status for CKD-relevant vaccines. Flag due/overdue. Prioritize Hep B for transplant candidates.
Interpret PHQ scores. Flag positive screens. Recommend treatment/referral per PHQ-9 severity. Note cognitive screen if applicable.
Assess fall risk integrating physical performance data (grip, STS, gait) with medication review and environmental factors. Recommend interventions.
Assess sleep apnea status and CPAP compliance. Note impact on CKD and CV risk. Recommend sleep study if high STOP-BANG and no prior diagnosis.
Assess social determinants of health. Flag barriers to care (transportation, food, housing, literacy). Recommend social work referral if needed.

Correlate physical performance with eGFR, anemia, fluid status. Flag high-risk combinations (low grip + declining eGFR + anemia). Track trends visit-to-visit. Recommend renal rehab referral when indicated. Adjust interpretation for confounders (edema affects gait, anemia affects endurance).
Assess nutritional status: protein intake adequacy, electrolyte-driven restrictions, plant-based diet discussion, malnutrition screening. Recommend dietitian if needed.

Assess overall medication adherence. Identify barriers (cost, complexity, access). Cross-reference with 4 Pillars sections. If medication gap exists, check barriers before recommending escalation. Note pregnancy/contraception if applicable.

sections.condition: patient enrolled in or candidate for specialty clinic
sections.interpretation_prompt: Document enrollment in specialty clinics (Cardiorenal Metabolic, Longevity, Heart Failure). Note referral status and outcomes.
Summarize care team, follow-up plan, referrals placed, education delivered, and billing complexity for the encounter.

cross_cutting_alerts.trigger: Patient is on RAAS inhibitor (ACEi/ARB/ARNi) AND diuretic AND NSAID
cross_cutting_alerts.action: Red alert in digital note builder + flag in medication adherence section
